title: Basic CRUD — Note API
description:
### Your Task

Build a complete REST API for managing Notes. One more rep of the CRUD pattern.

### Requirements

- Create a `Note` entity with fields: `id` (Long, auto-generated), `title` (String), `content` (String), `author` (String), `pinned` (Boolean)
- Create a `NoteRepository` extending `JpaRepository`
- Create a `NoteController` at `/api/notes` with CRUD endpoints

### Endpoints

- `GET /api/notes` → Return all notes (200 OK)
- `GET /api/notes/{id}` → Return note by ID (200 OK, or 404 if not found)
- `POST /api/notes` → Create note (201 Created)
- `PUT /api/notes/{id}` → Update note (200 OK, or 404 if not found)
- `DELETE /api/notes/{id}` → Delete note (204 No Content, or 404 if not found)

### Key Concepts

- Same pattern as Challenges 01 and 02
- This should feel faster now — you know the drill!
hints: Don't forget @RequestBody on POST and PUT
Use ResponseEntity for full control over status codes
Boolean fields work just like any other field in JPA
